mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.0% signals monetary accommodation that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rotate into equities seeking yield.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 1.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate above 6.0% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as growth expectations contract.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE > 6.0%, or correlation between UNRATE changes and forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth measured by RSAFS combined with housing starts measured by HOUST both declining simultaneously signals weakening consumer demand and construction activity, indicating recessionary pressure that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month decline > 1.0% AND HOUST month-over-month decline > 5.0%
failure_condition: Joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns in more than 45% of triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index INDPRO rising above 106.0 signals robust supply-side capacity utilization and manufacturing strength, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as investors anticipate earnings growth from operational efficiency.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 52% of instances when INDPRO > 106.0, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment measured by PAYEMS combined with declining unemployment rate UNRATE signals tight labor market conditions that increase wage pressures and corporate cost burdens, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as profit margins compress.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 0.3% AND UNRATE month-over-month decline > 0.2pp
failure_condition: Simultaneous PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline fail to predict negative returns in more than 48% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.20).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rising above 265.0 combined with elevated federal funds rate FEDFUNDS above 3.0% signals monetary tightening cycle that increases real discount rates and reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Simultaneous high inflation and high rates fail to predict negative returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or interaction term adds no explanatory power beyond individual series (R² improvement < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured by GDPC1 expanding at an annualized rate above 2.5% signals sustained economic expansion that supports corporate earnings and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as growth expectations remain elevated.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 45% of instances when GDPC1 growth > 2.5%, or correlation between GDP growth and forward returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts measured by HOUST declining below 1500 thousand units signals deteriorating residential construction demand and forward-looking economic weakness, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as investors anticipate broader demand contraction.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when HOUST < 1500, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
